samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="state" width_description="[1:0]">
            <enum-value id="ZERO" description="State ZERO">0</enum-value>
            <enum-value id="ONE" description="State ONE">1</enum-value>
        </enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">
            <reg-enum id="state" description="Current state of the FSM"/>
        </reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" width_description="[1:0]">
            <reg-enum id="next_state" description="Next state of the FSM"/>
        </reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
        <sequential>
            On each positive edge of the clock signal clk, the current state of the FSM is updated to the next state. 
            If the reset signal rst_n is low, the state is set to ZERO. 
            If the state is ZERO and the input signal a is high, the next state is ONE. 
            If the state is ONE and the input signal a is low, the next state is ZERO. 
            If the state is ONE and the input signal a is high, the rise output signal is set to 1. 
            If the state is ZERO and the input signal a is low, the down output signal is set to 1. 
            Otherwise, both output signals are set to 0.
        </sequential>
    </implementation>
</module>
